initialization: deterministic farthest-first k-means on each train fold
testInference: causal filtered posterior initialized from the final train posterior; no backward smoothing on test
stateMapping: Select the state with highest Laplace-smoothed NBER recession rate using train labels only; ties are deterministic.
selectionPolicy: No state count, covariance floor, transition prior, mapping rule or aggregation policy may change after OOS execution.
limitations: NBER supplies only a binary US recession label and cannot validate the full latent-state taxonomy.
Only two unique OOS recession months are available.
The 2008-2025 benchmark is development/validation and not a fresh final holdout.
